mission.title: argo: Helm chart + values files from Git
mission.description: Helm chart + values files from Git. This issue affects 434+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo deploy symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Helm chart + values files from Git

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
Hello everyone,

we are trying to migrate our CD processes to ArgoCD and faced an issue where I really need some input. (Checked the official documentation and threads here but seems like there is no

steps.title: Apply the fix for Helm chart + values files from Git
steps.description:
#2558 Based on discussions from:

https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/2558
https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/2789
https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/1105
https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/1153

I've spent a lot of time to put everything together to get integration wi
```yaml
# NOTE: this hasn't been tested and almost certainly doesn't work
configManagementPlugins: |
  - name: myrepo-charts
    init:
      command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
      args: 
      - |
        helm init --client-only --skip-refresh \
        && helm repo rm stable \
        && helm repo add myrepo https://artifactory.example.com/artifactory/helm \
        && helm repo update \
        && helm fetch --untar --untardir charts --version $CHART_VERSION myrepo/$CHART_NAME

    generate:
      command: [sh, -c]
      args: ["helm template charts/$CHART_NAME -f $CHART_VALUES"]
```

steps.title: Confirm Helm chart + values files from Git is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: #2558 Based on discussions from:

https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/2558
https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/2789
https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/1105
https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/1153

I've spent a lot of time to put everything together to get integration with GCS working. I think it's worth to have documentation for it.

Checklist:

* [X] Either (a) I've . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
# NOTE: this hasn't been tested and almost certainly doesn't work
configManagementPlugins: |
  - name: myrepo-charts
    init:
      command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
      args: 
      - |
        helm init --client-only --skip-refresh \
        && helm repo rm stable \
        && helm repo add myrepo https://artifactory.example.com/artifactory/helm \
        && helm repo update \
        && helm fetch --untar --untardir charts --version $CHART_VERSION myrepo/$CHART_NAME

    generate:
      command: [sh, -c]
      args: ["helm template charts/$CHART_NAME -f $CHART_VALUES"]

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: myapp-dit
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://bitbucket.example.com/scm/foo/myapp.git
    targetRevision: HEAD
    path: ""
    helm:
      repoURL: https://artifactory.example.com/artifactory/helm
      chart: "web-service"
      chartVersion: "1.0.0"
      valueFiles: ['values-dit.yaml'] # resolved against source.repoURL and source.path

data:
  configManagementPlugins: |
    - name: helm-resolver
      init:
        command: [bash, -c]
        args: ["helm repo add <repoName> <repoURL>
      generate:
        command: [bash, -c]
        args: ["helm template -n $ARGOCD_APP_NAMESPACE --values values.yaml $ARGOCD_APP_NAME <chart> --version CHART-VERSION"]

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
